title: ALIGN: Open-Source Analog Layout Automation from the Ground Up
abstract: We present ALIGN, an open-source, fully automated analog layout tool that translates SPICE netlists to design-rule-correct GDS layouts. ALIGN encodes layout symmetry, device pairing, common-centroid placement, and shielding constraints extracted directly from the netlist topology.

title: MAGICAL: Toward Fully Automated Analog IC Layout Leveraging Human and Machine Intelligence
abstract: MAGICAL is a fully automated analog IC layout system that combines human design knowledge encoded as layout constraints with machine intelligence for placement and routing. The system generates DRC-clean layouts from SPICE netlists targeting advanced FinFET processes.

title: OpenFASoC: Fully Open-Source Autonomous SoC Synthesis using FASoC Analog-Block Generation
abstract: OpenFASoC is an open-source framework for fully autonomous analog/mixed-signal SoC generation. It produces complete GDS layouts and extracted netlists from high-level specifications using the open-source SKY130 PDK, targeting temperature sensors, LDOs, and other common analog blocks.

title: A Mixed-Signal VLSI Design Methodology with a Python-Based Schematic/Layout API
abstract: Berkeley Analog Generator (BAG2) introduces a Python-based framework for designing process-portable analog and mixed-signal circuits. Designers parameterize schematic and layout generators in Python; BAG handles technology-specific instantiation and connectivity across PDKs.

title: LAYGO2: A Python Package for Layout Automation of Custom IC Designs
abstract: LAYGO2 is a layout automation framework that represents cell layouts as grid-based objects and composes them through Python APIs. It supports advanced FinFET processes and generates DRC-clean GDS layouts for custom analog and digital cells.

title: DREAMPlace: Deep Learning Toolkit-Enabled GPU Acceleration for Modern VLSI Placement
abstract: DREAMPlace recasts analytical VLSI placement as a deep learning problem, enabling GPU-accelerated gradient-based optimization. The framework achieves orders-of-magnitude speedup over CPU-based placers while producing placements of competitive quality for both standard-cell and mixed-size designs.

title: AutoCkt: Deep Reinforcement Learning of Analog Circuit Designs
abstract: AutoCkt applies deep reinforcement learning to automate analog circuit sizing. A trained RL agent navigates the design space of transistor sizes and bias currents to meet user-specified performance targets, demonstrating transfer learning across circuit topologies.

title: CircuitNet: An Open-Source Dataset for Machine Learning Applications in Electronic Design Automation
abstract: CircuitNet is a large-scale open-source dataset containing over 20,000 chip designs generated from commercial EDA tools, providing routing congestion maps, IR drop, DRC violations, and timing information. It benchmarks ML models for physical design prediction tasks.

title: Toward an Open-Source Digital Flow: First Learnings from the OpenROAD Project
abstract: OpenROAD presents a fully autonomous, open-source RTL-to-GDS flow that eliminates the need for human intervention. The project integrates synthesis, floorplanning, placement, clock tree synthesis, routing, and sign-off verification in a single push-button flow.

title: TritonRoute: The Open-Source Detailed Router
abstract: TritonRoute is an open-source detailed router for advanced-node VLSI designs. It employs a MILP-based panel routing engine with intra-layer and inter-layer routing conflict resolution, supporting multi-cut via optimization and complex design rule constraints.

title: Attention Routing: Track-Assignment Detailed Routing Using Attention-Based Deep Reinforcement Learning
abstract: This work applies attention-based deep reinforcement learning to track-assignment detailed routing. The agent learns routing policies from successful routing solutions and generalizes to unseen netlists, achieving high routing completion rates on industrial benchmarks.

title: OpenVAF: An Open-Source Verilog-A Compiler for Compact Device Models
abstract: OpenVAF is a modern Verilog-A compiler targeting compact device model development. It compiles Verilog-A model descriptions to OSDI-compatible shared libraries that can be loaded directly by circuit simulators such as ngspice and XYCE without requiring proprietary tools.

title: A Graph Neural Network Approach for Analog Circuits Sizing and Optimization
abstract: This paper formulates analog circuit sizing as a graph-based optimization problem and trains a GNN on a large corpus of SPICE simulation results. The trained GNN predicts circuit performance from device sizes, enabling gradient-based optimization that is orders of magnitude faster than SPICE-in-the-loop approaches.

title: GDSFactory: An Open-Source Python Package for Semiconductor Device Design
abstract: GDSFactory provides a component-based Python API for designing photonic integrated circuits and semiconductor devices. Designs are composed from parameterized cells, verified with built-in DRC/simulation hooks, and exported to GDS, OASIS, or fabrication-ready formats.

title: PROBE3.0: A Systematic Framework for Design-Technology Pathfinding with Improved Design Enablement
abstract: PROBE3.0 is a framework for systematic design-technology pathfinding that co-optimizes standard cell library, PDK design rules, and routing resource allocation. It provides open benchmarks and metrics for evaluating the impact of technology scaling on PPA.

title: CACE: A Framework for Automated Analog/Mixed-Signal Circuit Characterization and Evaluation
abstract: CACE automates the characterization and specification verification of analog and mixed-signal circuits. Given a schematic and a spec sheet, CACE sweeps conditions, runs SPICE simulations, and produces pass/fail reports against each specification corner.
